title: Re-using pipette tips for gel loading
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9gdco","text":"Gel loading tips can be reused for most applications; there’s REALLY no need to throw them away after a single use. In\nour labs, we routinely reuse tips when loading gels, only switching to fresh ones when we plan to extract bands for cloning or sequencing.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cokfn","text":" ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Genoyping Pipette tips can be REused for multiple rounds of genotyping saving thousands of tips in your lab. Watch Anya demonstrate how to load a gel with a much smaller number of pipette tips. Don’t forget to keep your box of tips for subsequent rounds of genotyping, they can be used again and again!

Our adorable mini gel rig was made by laser cutting pieces from an old broken gel rig. It uses three times less agarose than the equivalent 5 lane commericial gel rig. Saving agarose and cute toboot! Feeling inspired? Download the laser-cutter pattern and build your own sustainable rig.

Checking your DNA or RNA You can just use one tip to load a gel. Rinsing the tip with the running buffer between samples is an efficient way to avoid contamination. The only time we use a fresh tip is if we want to extract the band for cloning or sequencing.

Mapping mutants We have mapped multiple mutants re-using the same tips again and again (e.g. Cachd1 ; Tcf7l1a ; mab21l2 u517 ). Again, just rinse your tips between samples and at the end ,then store for loading the next gel. This tip comes from Will Talbots lab at Stanford University where they have successfully mapped multiple zebrafish mutants using the same tips over and over again.
Keep designated pipette tips for solutions you use regularly. Really think beforeyou reach for a fresh tip! For more ways to store your pipette tips check out https://zebrafishucl.org/plastic
creator: Kate Turner
